pages.raw_text:
To the Public: Railroad adher-
ents and the Citizens committee
are arguing that the city of Spo-
kane cannot secure terminal rates
by the franchise method, because
the railroads are so powerful and
influential and have so much
money to spend, that the city of
Spokane cannot cope with them.


There seems to be no doubt what
ever that the city can refuse to
give a franchise to any railroad
and the discretion reposed in the
city council to exercise such re-
fusal cannot be questioned by a
court. 1t follows, necessarily. that
the city of Spokane can refuse to
grant a franchise except upon such
terms as are satisfactory to it.
The city can, therefore, secure a
just and equitable contract, or re-
fuse to enter into a contract. If
the city of Spokane secures a just
and equitable contract, it will re-
suit in the Inland Empire securing
the same benefits. This is fight
not only for Spokane, but for the
Inland Empire.


The jobberS in 1904. by concert
ed action. did secure from these
powerful railroads valuable conces-
sions and jobbing territory. Since
the jobbers secured these results
from the powerful railroads, this
city can, by sustaining the posi-
zion of tho citv council Cacl1ra hot
ter results than did the jobbers.
There is no railroad already in the
city which can handle the addi-
tional traffic of the Milwaukee, if
it secures that which it expects to
secure here. to-wit, not less than
25 per cent thereof.


We do not believe that the Mil
waukee has the right to make any
arrangements to come into the city
